v1_verdicts.title: There is no +error.
v1_verdicts.summary: Mechanically airtight: find returns zero +error.svelte files under src/routes and no src/error.html; no handleError in src/hooks.server.ts or hooks.client.ts. So every thrown error renders SvelteKit's unbranded default with no recovery affordance. The archive pre-publish 404 is real: archive/[token]/+page.server.ts throws 404 'Archive not found' (bad token, line 21; archive_enabled false, line 25) and 404 'Archive not yet published' when now < end_date + archive_publish_after_days (lines 33-35) — so a link shared at trip end 404s for the whole publish-delay window (default 7 days). Inbox 403 for non-owners verified at inbox/+page.server.ts:10-12. P1 stands per charter §5's explicit 'front-door (invite/join/archive) failure'; M2 one-way street is the right test.
v1_verdicts.evidence: find src/routes -name '+error.svelte' → none; no src/error.html; no handleError hooks; src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:14-35 (three 404 throws incl. pre-publish window math); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/inbox/+page.server.ts:10-12 (403).

v1_verdicts.title: Offline mode — a Trip-Mode-critical control ("I'm on the plane") — is a manual localStorage toggle buried in More, which is only reachable from the PLANNING nav.
v1_verdicts.summary: Toggle location (more/+page.svelte:145-176, localStorage + SET_OFFLINE + reload), trip nav without More (nav-tabs.ts:19-29: Now/Today/Add/Docs), and Documents auto-precache on active trips (documents/+page.svelte:44-51, onTrip-gated) all verified by content. Not documented-deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG's S5 offline precache (#74) shipped for Documents; no entry defers an in-trip offline control or page caching. P2 correct per charter: shipped-but-buried with the old stack (camera-roll screenshots) as the workaround; V3 seam cite valid since the mode pill is the only path and offline-on-the-plane is today-scoped (D3's consequence classifies mode-switch answers to today-scoped needs as dead ends).
v1_verdicts.amendment: All cited facts verified; one mechanism claim corrected — the finding UNDERSTATES the problem. Its NB says 'the toggle governs page/data caching'. Code: SET_OFFLINE flips a flag the service worker consults only in the PocketBase-API branch (service-worker.ts:89-103, port 8090 or /api/ paths). SvelteKit navigations are network-first with a fallback to a cache nothing ever populates (no cache.put for navigations, lines 128-139), and __data.json requests hit the final cache-first branch which also never puts (142-146). SSR pages load data server-side, so the toggle effectively gates almost nothing a member's browser does. The control is therefore both unreachable in Trip Mode AND nearly inert — strengthens the finding's own fix (make caching automatic, demote the toggle).
v1_verdicts.evidence: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte:145-176; src/lib/shell/nav-tabs.ts:19-29; src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/documents/+page.svelte:44-51; src/service-worker.ts:19-39 (SET_OFFLINE), 89-146 (per-branch behavior, no nav/__data puts); docs/SPEC_BACKLOG.md:157,164.

new_findings.title: Suggestion flow is fire-and-forget: traveler never sees status or outcome
new_findings.note: Replaces refuted WP-A-008. Verified severity carried by the WP-A-009 amendment (→P1): silent overview redirect with no toast; traveler-scoped suggestions list API shipped-but-unreachable (M3); no approve/reject notification type; created_by attribution lands on the reviewer, not the author.
new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/items/new + /trips/[slug]/inbox — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/new/+page.server.ts:228-250 (suggestion submit redirects 303 to trip overview), items/new/+page.svelte:79-89 (enhance callback has no success feedback), inbox/+page.server.ts:10-12 (403 for non-owners), backend/pb_hooks/suggestions.pb.js:112-116 (list API comment: 'traveler sees own' — no UI calls it), backend/pb_hooks/notifications.pb.js:4-7 (only suggestion_added/comment_added/member_joined types exist)
new_findings.what: On a trip with auto-approve off, a traveler submits an item via the only add path (items/new). The pre-submit banner warns it will be reviewed, but after submit they are silently redirected to the trip Overview with no confirmation toast, the suggestion appears nowhere they can reach (Inbox 403s travelers even though GET /api/suggestions/list explicitly supports 'traveler sees own' — shipped backend capability with zero frontend surface, M3), and no notification type exists for approve/reject, so the outcome never reaches them. Bonus attribution drop: on approve, suggestions.pb.js sets item.created_by to the REVIEWING owner, not the traveler author.
new_findings.why: V2 (silences a contributor): Scott's mother suggests a restaurant, then sees nothing — did it send? Was it rejected? She texts the group to ask, which is the exact old-stack failure. Also M3: the traveler-scoped suggestions list API is shipped-but-unreachable. Charter §5 lists 'contributor-silencing gap' as P1. Not documented-deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG Collaboration backlog covers notification dedup/realtime but not review-outcome notification or a traveler-facing status surface; SPEC.md:610 only requires the pre-submit warning, which exists.
new_findings.fix: Three small pieces: (1) success toast/confirmation after suggestion submit ('Sent to [owner] for review'); (2) a traveler-visible 'My suggestions' section (e.g. on the trip Overview or a non-403 inbox view) reading the existing traveler-scoped list API; (3) a suggestion_reviewed notification to the author on approve/reject in suggestions.pb.js review handler, and set created_by to the suggestion author on approve.

new_findings.title: Phase-less unplanned items land in an invisible black hole

verified.summary: All three creation paths and the invisibility both verified. Creation: (a) ItemForm offers Day '' (Unscheduled, :410) and Phase '' (None, :433) with no server-side phase requirement and status = day?'planned':'unplanned'; (b) the suggestions hook's auto-approve and review-approve paths both write phase:''/day:''/status:'unplanned' when the payload lacks them; (c) phases.pb.js onRecordDeleteRequest re-buckets only days' phases multi-relation and never touches items, while 0006_items.js declares items.phase without cascadeDelete, so PB clears item.phase on phase delete — orphaning that phase's entire parking lot. Invisibility: every parking surface is phase-scoped (day view filters i.phase === p.id; phase detail queries phase = id; trip-wide /parking-lot is a 303 to /phases, retired #86; swipe is per-phase), violating CONTEXT.md's 'unplanned… phase required' invariant. Not deferred — SPEC_BACKLOG:29 reasserts 'Every item requires a phase' and TRIP_REPLANNING_PRD's skip door preserves phase. P2 right (M3 unreachable state reading as data loss; not a critical-path break).
verified.evidence: src/lib/itinerary/components/ItemForm.svelte:410/:433 (claim said :428 — minor drift, content true); items/new/+page.server.ts:~264 (status ternary, no phase validation); backend/pb_hooks/suggestions.pb.js:79-98, :264-298; backend/pb_hooks/phases.pb.js:79-110 (days only); backend/pb_migrations/0006_items.js:14 (no cascadeDelete); days/[dayId]/+page.svelte:142 (i.phase === p.id); phases/[phaseId]/+page.server.ts:32; parking-lot/+page.server.ts (303 redirect); CONTEXT.md:14,:46; docs/SPEC_BACKLOG.md:29.

new_findings.where: src/lib/itinerary/components/ItemForm.svelte:410 (Day 'Unscheduled') + :428 (Phase 'None'), src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/new/+page.server.ts:264 (status = day ? 'planned' : 'unplanned' with no phase requirement), backend/pb_hooks/suggestions.pb.js approve path (item.set('status', payload.day ? 'planned' : 'unplanned'), phase may be ''), backend/pb_hooks/phases.pb.js onRecordDeleteRequest (re-buckets days only, never re-homes items), backend/pb_migrations/0006_items.js:14 (phase relation, no cascade), src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/parking-lot/+page.server.ts (trip-wide parking page retired #86, redirects to /phases)
new_findings.what: An item with status=unplanned and phase='' is renderable nowhere: every parking-lot surface is phase-scoped (day-view ParkingDivider zones filter i.phase === p.id, Phase Detail filters its own items, the trip-wide parking page is a 303 redirect), day views need a day, the Overview lists only day summaries, and the swipe deck filters by phase. Three real paths create this state: (a) picking Phase 'None' + Day 'Unscheduled' in the item form (both options offered), (b) approving a suggestion whose payload has no day/phase, (c) deleting a phase — PocketBase nullifies items.phase and the delete hook re-homes nothing, so that phase's whole parking lot vanishes from every screen while still existing in the DB. Violates the CONTEXT.md invariant 'An unplanned item belongs to a phase.'
new_findings.why: M3 (shipped-but-unreachable state): the records exist but no route or view lists them; the only ways back are a remembered /items/[itemId] URL or export JSON. For the novice it reads as data loss — 'my idea disappeared' — and phase deletion silently eats N ideas with a two-tap 'Delete?' confirm that never mentions the consequence. Not documented anywhere as deferred (checked SPEC_BACKLOG Itinerary, CARD_CONTENT_SPEC, PHASE_REDESIGN_PRD).
new_findings.fix: Enforce the invariant: require a phase when day is Unscheduled in ItemForm + server validation (items/new and edit), make the suggestion-approve hook fall back to the trip's first phase or force a phase pick, and on phase delete either block while unplanned items exist ('Move 4 ideas first') or reassign them to a chosen phase. Optionally add an 'Unsorted ideas' catch-all section on the Phases sub-tab as a safety net for existing orphans.

new_findings.title: Edit & Approve silently discards the traveler's proposed day, phase, and cost

verified.summary: Every link in the claimed chain verified in code: the inbox Edit & Approve link carries no day/phase params, the server prefill passes the raw payload (incl. day/phase) but the client initialData maps only type/title/description/times/location/codes/booked/url/cancellation on top of buildEmptyFormData's day:''/phase:'' — so day, phase, subtype, end_date, cost_estimate_usd, assigned_to silently reset. On submit the review hook does `payload = modifiedPayload || rawPayload` (wholesale replacement), so 'Approve with edits' writes phase:''/day:''/status:'unplanned', discarding the traveler's scheduling intent and landing the item in the phase-less black hole; plain Approve (no payload) preserves the original. Not deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG:85 still says 'UI exposes no edit form' — stale, the UI shipped — and a backlog entry deferring the UI cannot shield a live data-dropping defect. P2 right (V2 drop at the review seam; flow completes, so not P1).
verified.evidence: inbox/+page.svelte:114-119 (?suggestion= only); items/new/+page.server.ts:42-66 (prefill = raw payload), :79-80 (preselectedDay/Phase from URL only); items/new/+page.svelte:41-54 (initialData omits day/phase/subtype/end_date/cost/assigned_to); src/lib/itinerary/item-fields.ts:180-181 (day:'', phase:''); backend/pb_hooks/suggestions.pb.js:264 (modifiedPayload || rawPayload), :269-270 + status from payload.day; docs/SPEC_BACKLOG.md:85.

new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/items/new?suggestion=… — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/new/+page.svelte:41-54 (initialData prefills only type/title/description/times/location/codes/booked/url/cancellation — omits day, phase, subtype, end_date, cost_estimate_usd, assigned_to), +page.server.ts:44-66 (prefill spread includes payload.day/phase but nothing reads them), inbox/+page.svelte:114-119 (Edit & Approve link carries no ?day= so preselectedDay/Phase are empty)
new_findings.what: The owner's Edit & Approve path opens the item form with Day reset to 'Unscheduled' and Phase to 'None' even when the traveler proposed a specific day/phase, and the cost estimate and subtype are likewise dropped. If the owner tweaks the title and hits 'Approve with edits', the modified payload (day='', phase='') overrides the original in /api/suggestions/review, so the approved item loses its scheduling intent — and lands in the phase-less unplanned black hole (separate finding). Plain 'Approve' preserves the payload; only the edit path corrupts it.
new_findings.why: V2 (drops a contributor's input at the review seam — the traveler said 'Tuesday dinner', the approved item says nothing) and a mechanical data-handling defect on a shipped flow. SPEC_BACKLOG's 'Edit-and-approve inbox UI' backlog entry is stale (the UI shipped), so this is live behavior, not deferred scope.
new_findings.fix: Prefill day, phase, subtype, end_date, cost_estimate_usd, and assigned_to from the suggestion payload into initialData (the server already passes them in prefill); show a small 'proposed by X for [day]' context line so the owner sees what they are approving.

new_findings.title: Idea capture is buried two levels deep; trip home has no add affordance at all
new_findings.note: Unverified (2 finders, planning slice). Relates charter scenarios 2 + 11.
new_findings.where: /trips/[slug] — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+page.svelte (no FAB/add button outside the empty state, lines 208-228), src/lib/shell/nav-tabs.ts:31-40 (planning nav = Itinerary/Money/Members/Docs/More, no Add — Trip Mode nav has one at :25), phases/[phaseId]/+page.svelte:163-167 (the only labeled '+ Add idea' quick capture, #57), days/[dayId]/+page.svelte:151-159+203 (day '+ Add' creates a planned item; parking requires switching Day→'Unscheduled' in the full form, ItemForm.svelte:410)
new_findings.what: Charter scenario 2 ('Add an idea I'm not sure about yet') for the novice: from the trip Overview there is no visible add button of any kind once content exists. The dedicated idea capture lives only on Phase Detail, reached via Overview → Phases sub-tab → tap a phase card → '+ Add idea' (or knowing the phase name on Overview is a link). The day-view FAB path is a trap for this intent: it pre-commits the idea to that day as a planned item, and parking it instead requires understanding that Day='Unscheduled' in a 10-field form means 'parking lot'. The words 'parking lot'/'ideas' appear nowhere on the Overview.
new_findings.why: V2: the moment of 'heard about a restaurant, jot it down before I forget' (scenario 11) costs 3+ taps behind vocabulary ('Phases') that doesn't advertise ideas; the realistic novice fallback is texting the group. D1 (no collaboration hub) is respected — this is about reachability of one capture surface, so severity capped at P2 per D1's ceiling, not the absence of a hub.
new_findings.fix: Give the planning Overview a lightweight add entry: either a FAB that opens a mini capture sheet (title + type + phase picker, defaulting to parking lot) or an 'Ideas' rollup row per phase section on the Overview linking to Phase Detail's capture. Keep the full form for scheduled items.

new_findings.title: Viewer role gets full write UI and the writes actually succeed

verified.summary: Could not refute any layer. UI: day-view '+ Add' link and FAB render unconditionally (days/[dayId]/+page.svelte ~151-159, 203); phases page 'Add phase' button and create/reorder/delete actions have zero role/membership-role references (grep of phases/+page.server.ts: membership used only for swipe-progress filter); item detail shows Move/Edit/Delete to all roles. Server: items/new action's only branch is submitAsSuggestion = role==='traveler' && !auto_approve — a viewer falls through to the direct-create path. Backend: migrations 0008 + 0014 set items and phases full CRUD to MEMBER_VIA_TRIP ('@request.auth.id != "" && trip.trip_members_via_trip.user ?= @request.auth.id') — no role term; no onRecord hooks exist for items, and phases.pb.js hooks contain no role checks. So viewer writes succeed end-to-end. The contrast pattern is real: tasks gate via an app-layer isViewer helper (items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts ~103-106 with a comment saying exactly why), documents.pb.js:36 and expenses.pb.js:30 deny viewers. RULES.md's intended matrix (items.update/delete: viewer '—', traveler '—') and the M2d 'tighten items.create' plan confirm intent-never-landed (progress marker still at M2b). Not documented-deferred per the charter's closed list (SPEC_BACKLOG has only role-downgrade + documents permissions; RULES.md is not a charter-recognized deferral doc and is itself stale). P2 right: M4 expected-but-missing gate / shipped role model contradicted — not on the charter's P1 critical-path list.
verified.evidence: items/new/+page.server.ts (~228 traveler-only suggestion branch, direct-create comment omits viewer); backend/pb_migrations/0008_tighten_rules.js:30-47 + 0014_explicit_rules_audit.js:54-83 (MEMBER_VIA_TRIP, 'full CRUD by trip members'); no item hooks, phases.pb.js role-free; isViewer gate in items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts; backend/RULES.md matrix + M2d; docs/SPEC.md:83-87; SPEC_BACKLOG.md:82,165.

new_findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/days/[dayId]/+page.svelte:151-159,203 (+Add link and FAB render for all roles), phases/+page.svelte:73-76,188-262 (Add phase / reorder / delete unconditional), items/[itemId]/+page.svelte:54-72,330-377 (Move/Edit/Delete for all roles), items/new/+page.server.ts:88-96 (no role gate; viewer takes the direct-create branch since submitAsSuggestion is traveler-only), backend/pb_migrations/0008_tighten_rules.js:40-47 + 0014_explicit_rules_audit.js (items/phases rules = any trip member), backend/RULES.md:37 vs :79-80 (live matrix 'member of trip' vs intended 'items.update/delete: owner/co-owner only; viewer: —')
new_findings.what: A viewer — the read-only role the Members page mints join links for — sees every planning write affordance (FAB, +Add, Add/delete phase, Edit/Move/Delete item) and, because PB rules for items/phases/days are member-of-trip with no role check and the SvelteKit actions add none, the writes succeed. Only checklists/tasks, goals, votes, and documents gate viewers (via hooks/rules). RULES.md's own intended-permissions matrix (lines 79-80) says viewers can do none of this and travelers shouldn't update/delete items directly; the 'tighten items.create' step planned at the M2d marker (RULES.md:95) never landed.
new_findings.why: M4 (expected-but-missing gate between shipped role model and shipped UI): the viewer role's entire premise — safe link for a non-participant — is false; grandma-the-viewer can delete a phase with two taps. RULES.md documents the intent but no SPEC_BACKLOG/PRD entry defers the enforcement, and the UI contradicts the role pill shown on Members.
new_findings.fix: Two layers: hide write affordances from viewers in the planning surfaces (day FAB/+Add, phases CRUD, item Edit/Move/Delete — same pattern as goals' canAdd), and add the role gate server-side (hook-based viewer deny on items/phases writes, the pattern RULES.md established for invites/goals).

new_findings.title: Suggestion review gate covers only item creation — edit/move/delete and phase mutations open to every role

verified.summary: Verified every cited action: items/[itemId]/edit update action (read in full around lines 53-184) fetches the item and writes with no membership/role lookup; items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts delete (~121-137) and moveItem (~417-433) likewise; phases/+page.server.ts create/reorder/delete have no role checks; days/[dayId]/+page.server.ts updateNotes (~66-78) writes day notes unguarded. PB rules are role-blind for all of these (0014: items/phases full CRUD = member-via-trip, days.update = member-via-trip). Meanwhile the SAME item-detail server file gates every checklist/task action through isViewer with an explicit comment that PB rules are membership-scoped so the viewer gate lives at the app layer — proving the intended pattern exists and these doors were skipped, not designed open. The traveler asymmetry is real: items/new routes traveler→suggestion when auto-approve is off, but a traveler can edit/move/delete any existing item directly, hollowing the review funnel. SPEC.md:83-87 verified (traveler 'suggest only*', viewer '—'), and the charter's known-stale list covers nav + Vault rows, not these. P2 confirmed. Note for Phase 3: this and the viewer-write finding are two facets of one root cause (role-blind items/phases/days writes) — file as one fix with two symptom statements, not two issues.
verified.evidence: items/[itemId]/edit/+page.server.ts update action (no role check); items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts delete + moveItem (none) vs isViewer-gated task actions in same file; phases/+page.server.ts (grep role/isOwner/viewer → only swipe-progress membership use); days/[dayId]/+page.server.ts:66-78; 0014_explicit_rules_audit.js:54-83; docs/SPEC.md:83-87; item detail + phases page chrome ungated.

new_findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/[itemId]/edit/+page.server.ts:53-184 (update action: no role check); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts:121-137 (delete), :417-433 (moveItem) — no role checks, while the same file's checklist/task actions all gate viewers (:103-106,196,225,244…); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/phases/+page.server.ts:32-133 (create/reorder/delete: no role check); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/days/[dayId]/+page.server.ts:66-78 (updateNotes: no role check); backend/pb_migrations/0014_explicit_rules_audit.js:76-83 (items full CRUD = any trip member, role-blind); docs/SPEC.md:83-87 (permissions table: viewer '—' for items, traveler 'suggest only*'); chrome: items/[itemId]/+page.svelte:54-71 (Edit/Move shown to all roles), phases/+page.svelte:73-75 (Add phase shown to all)
new_findings.what: With auto-approve OFF a traveler must queue a NEW item through owner review, yet can directly edit, move, or delete any EXISTING item (and create/delete whole phases, edit day notes) — no app-layer role check and PB items/phases/days rules are role-blind (any member via trip). Viewers — read-only per SPEC.md's permission table — get working Edit/Move/Delete buttons and the writes succeed. Meanwhile checklists, tasks, goals, lists, and booking actions all carefully gate viewers, proving the intended pattern exists.
new_findings.why: Guard-vs-chrome/spec contradiction (M-axis): the review funnel is enforced at exactly one door while the walls are open; SPEC.md §permissions (charter doc-precedence #6, and these rows are not in the known-stale list) says otherwise. Undermines the auto-approve setting's promise to the owner.
new_findings.fix: Add the viewer (and, when auto-approve is off, traveler-vs-others policy decision) guards to items edit/delete/moveItem, phases create/reorder/delete, and day updateNotes — or tighten PB rules per CLAUDE.md 'PB rules first' and surface role-appropriate chrome (hide Edit/Move/Delete for viewers).

new_findings.title: moveItem action desyncs status from day: one-tap paths to invisible or double-rendered items

verified.summary: Reproduced fully in code. moveItem (items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts, last action) updates exactly {day, phase} — no status touch, no time-stripping. The sibling edit action contains the precise invariant ('Status logic' comment: !day && status not done/considered → unplanned; day && unplanned → planned), so drift-not-design is accurate. MoveItemSheet offers 'No day (unscheduled)' and 'No phase' options to any item. State (a): planned item moved to no-day matches neither the day timeline query (filter day="id" && end_date="") nor any parking surface (all filter status='unplanned') → invisible on day/phase surfaces, consistent with the claimed URL/goal-link/booking-list-only findability. State (b): unplanned item moved onto a day matches the timeline (day match, no status filter) AND the day-page parking query (status='unplanned' + phase match, no day exclusion) and phase-detail parking (phaseItems.filter(status==='unplanned')) → double-rendered. The reorder guard exists verbatim: phases/[phaseId]/+page.server.ts rejects moved.day !== '' with 'Only this phase's ideas can be reordered.' — the code half-knows the state is illegal, as claimed. Not in SPEC_BACKLOG or any PRD deferred list. P2 fits: shipped Move feature manufactures states that read as data loss (M3), but not on a charter P1 critical path.
verified.evidence: items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts moveItem (day+phase only) vs edit/+page.server.ts status-reconciliation block; src/lib/itinerary/components/MoveItemSheet.svelte:~68 ('No day (unscheduled)') + ~85 ('No phase'); days/[dayId]/+page.server.ts:23-26 (timeline filter) + ~55-61 (parking filter, no day exclusion); phases/[phaseId]/+page.svelte:20 (status-only parking filter); phases/[phaseId]/+page.server.ts:~157-159 (reorder guard, exact error string).

Two broken states result: (a) planned item moved to 'No day (unscheduled)' keeps status=planned → appears on NO day page, NO parking lot (wrong status), NO swipe deck slot it should occupy; it is findable only by URL, goal back-link, or the booking smart list if it happens to require booking. The sheet's own label says 'unscheduled', which to the user means parking lot. (b) Unplanned idea moved onto a day keeps status=unplanned → renders BOTH in that day's timeline AND as a parking suggestion on every day of its phase, and the phase-detail drag-reorder then rejects it with 'Only this phase's ideas can be reordered.'

Not in SPEC_BACKLOG or any PRD deferred list.

Optionally have the day-page parking query exclude day != '' as a render-side belt-and-braces.

new_findings.title: Trip-Mode quick-add ejects to Planning on save and on back — `from=trip` is a dead param

verified.summary: Fully reproduced in code. AddSheet.svelte:15-20 constructs ?from=trip&day=<todayId>; items/new/+page.server.ts reads only day/phase/suggestion (lines 14-16) — `from` is read nowhere in the repo, a ghost param proving the intended return path (M4). Success with a day redirects to /trips/[slug]/days/[day] (line 273-274) — verified a Planning surface (days/[dayId] contains DayNav/parking/drag); cancel/back is NavBar backHref=/trips/[slug] (items/new/+page.svelte:57); the traveler-with-auto-approve-off path redirects bare to /trips/[slug] (line 249) with no toast/param/confirmation anywhere. Because AppShell (mounted at trips/[slug]/+layout.svelte) keeps mode='trip' during an active trip, the Planning day editor renders under stale trip chrome with 'Now' highlighted (getActiveTab fallback) — the desync claim holds. Not deferred: TRIP_REPLANNING_PRD.md Out of Scope excludes quick-add precisely because 'the Add sheet already covers quick-add-for-today' — an assumption this redirect falsifies, so no other workstream owns the fix. P1 stands per binding D3: scenario 22 is today-scoped, 'must not leave Trip Mode', and D3 pre-classifies a today-scoped task answered by a Planning surface as a dead end on the mid-trip critical path; the central oversized Add button is Trip Mode's primary action.
verified.evidence: AddSheet.svelte:15-20; items/new/+page.server.ts:14-16 (params read), 249, 273-276 (redirects); items/new/+page.svelte:57 (backHref); grep 'from' param reads → zero; days/[dayId]/* match DayNav/parking/drag; AppShell.svelte:35-44 (mode derivation, in-memory override) + nav-tabs.ts getActiveTab trip-branch fallback 'now'; docs/TRIP_REPLANNING_PRD.md Out of Scope bullet + Further Notes (WP-A-005 origin).

new_findings.where: AddSheet → /trips/[slug]/items/new — src/lib/trip-mode/components/AddSheet.svelte:15-20; src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/new/+page.server.ts:273-276 (success redirect → /trips/[slug]/days/[dayId]) and :249 (traveler-suggestion path → /trips/[slug]); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/new/+page.svelte:57 (NavBar back → /trips/[slug])
new_findings.what: The Trip-Mode central Add button's 'Add item to today' navigates to items/new with ?from=trip&day=<todayId>, but no code anywhere reads the `from` param (repo-wide grep: only AddSheet constructs it). On save the action redirects to the Planning day view (/days/[dayId] — drag handles, parking lot, DayNav); cancel/back goes to the Planning trip overview. A traveler with auto-approve off is redirected to the Planning overview with no confirmation of what happened to their item. Every exit from the one-handed mid-trip quick-add lands on a Planning surface (under stale trip chrome, since AppShell mode state persists — see the mode-desync finding).
new_findings.why: M4 (expected-but-missing return link — the `from=trip` ghost param proves the return path was intended and never wired) and charter D3/V3: quick-add for today is Light Replanning and 'must work inside Trip Mode'; D3 pre-classifies a today-scoped task whose answer is a Planning surface as a dead end. Fails scenario 22 (and the landing half of 20). NOT documented-deferred: docs/TRIP_REPLANNING_PRD.md:72 explicitly excludes quick-add from the doors PRD because 'the Add sheet already covers quick-add-for-today' — an assumption this redirect falsifies.
new_findings.fix: Honor the param: when from=trip (or a returnTo param), redirect success to /trips/[slug]/today and set the form's backHref to /trips/[slug]/today; give the traveler-suggestion path the same return plus an in-context 'sent for review' confirmation. Longer term, consider a lighter trip-scoped quick-add sheet instead of the full planning ItemForm.

new_findings.title: Offline surface is document-bytes-only: no app shell, navigations and __data are never cached — offline relaunch is a 503 dead end

verified.summary: Every mechanical sub-claim holds: the service worker never caches navigation HTML or __data.json (the only cache.put calls are docs precache :33, docs cache-on-view :78, and PB-API responses :111), 'prerendered' is not imported and no route sets prerender, so the navigate fallback (:128-139) always misses → literal 'Offline' 503 text; the generic branch (:141-147) is cache-first with no put, so in-app nav offline dies too (fetch rejection → SvelteKit hard-nav → same 503). precacheDocuments has exactly one call site, gated on onTrip on the Documents page, so the cached boarding-pass bytes are unreachable in the state they exist for (M3). The More offline toggle only gates PB-API requests SSR pages never make, and its own reload() 503s offline. Not documented-deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG's only offline entries are notification-realtime and S5 docs-precache (itself shipped in code while still listed as backlog — extra doc-hygiene wrinkle); SPEC.md:498/511/687 affirmatively claim full offline read as shipped. P1 fits the charter bar exactly: mid-trip dead end on the critical path (scenarios 19/28), V1 back-to-email for the boarding pass.
verified.evidence: src/service-worker.ts full read (:17 ASSETS=build+files; :61-86 docs branch; :88-124 isPBApi=port 8090||/api/; :128-139 navigate, no put; :141-147 generic, no put); grep cache.put → lines 33/78/111 only; grep prerender in src/routes → none; precacheDocuments call sites → offline-cache.ts def + documents/+page.svelte:50 (inside $effect gated if(!onTrip) return); more/+page.svelte:148-156 (toggle → SET_OFFLINE + location.reload); docs/SPEC.md:498-499,511,687; docs/SPEC_BACKLOG.md:84,157,164.

new_findings.where: src/service-worker.ts:127-139 (navigate: network-first with a caches.match fallback that nothing ever populates → new Response('Offline', 503)), :141-147 (generic branch — __data.json — cache-first but never cache.put), :61-86 (only document file bytes get runtime caching), :88-124 (data cache keyed to port 8090/'/api/' — SSR page loads via locals.pb bypass the SW entirely); precache trigger: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/documents/+page.svelte:44-51 (fires only when the Documents page is visited while the trip is active); manual offline toggle: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte:147-155 calls window.location.reload(), which itself 503s when truly offline
new_findings.what: The S5 documents offline cache works at the bytes level (precache + cache-on-view, truthful tick), but there is no offline path to any UI that surfaces those bytes. Page HTML and SvelteKit __data.json responses are never written to any cache, so: (a) relaunching the PWA offline (normal on a plane — iOS evicts PWAs aggressively) yields a raw 'Offline' 503 text response, not the app; (b) any in-app navigation while offline fails its data fetch and degrades to the same 503. The cached boarding-pass PDF is unreachable unless the Documents page is already rendered and kept alive. The precache itself only fires on a Documents-page visit during the active window, so the day-1 flight — the single most offline-critical moment — is only covered if the user happened to open the Docs tab after start_date while still online. SPEC.md's offline contract ('Full trip works offline after one online load' :511, §Offline behavior :637-640, :687) describes the pre-SSR client-fetch architecture and no longer holds; the More-page 'go offline' toggle and SET_OFFLINE plumbing are vestigial against SSR loads.
new_findings.why: M3 — a shipped feature (offline document cache, V4_DOCUMENTS_PRD 'available offline for the active trip', Q7/D5) is unreachable in exactly the state it exists for; and V1 — at the gate with no signal, the traveler goes back to the email/airline app for the boarding pass. Fails scenarios 19 and 28 on the mid-trip critical path. Not documented-deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG lists only S3/S4 (clipboard, previews); no backlog entry covers the offline app shell, and SPEC.md actively claims broader offline than exists (doc-hygiene: live-doc lie).
new_findings.fix: Give the SW a navigation fallback it can actually serve: cache a minimal offline shell (or last-seen Documents page) at install/visit, cache.put successful navigations and __data.json for trip-mode + documents routes (network-first), and trigger the documents precache from any trip-mode page during the active window (or at activation), not only on a Documents-tab visit. Update SPEC.md's offline section to the real contract.

new_findings.title: Opening an active trip lands on the planning overview wearing Trip-Mode chrome with the 'Now' tab falsely lit
new_findings.note: Unverified (2 finders). The mode chimera: chrome is derived in memory, never from the route.
new_findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.svelte:70 ('On trip' card href=/trips/{slug}); src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte:35-44 (mode derived from trip status + in-memory override, never from URL); src/lib/shell/nav-tabs.ts:43-48 (trip-mode getActiveTab falls back to 'now' for every non-tab path); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+page.svelte:54-70 (planning overview content, moss SubTabs)
new_findings.what: Charter scenario 18 (what's next right now) + 44. The trips list's 'On trip' card links to /trips/[slug] — the planning itinerary overview. Because the trip is active, AppShell renders trip chrome (clay 4-tab nav), and getActiveTab's fallback lights 'Now' even though the content is the planning overview with Overview/Phases/Lists/Goals sub-tabs. The mode pill reads 'Edit plan' while she is literally looking at the plan-editing surface, and tapping it appears to do nothing but recolor the tabs (it navigates to the page she's already on, AppShell.svelte:48-55). CONTEXT.md and SPEC.md both say Trip Mode is 'the default mode for active trips' — the chrome obeys, the content doesn't. The clay 'Trip Mode' button on the stats card (+page.svelte:92-101) is a partial mitigation, but it goes to /today while the pill toggle goes to /now — two different 'homes'. This mixed state recurs on every hard reload/PWA relaunch mid-trip since the override is in-memory only (AppShell.svelte:38).
new_findings.why: M4 — the expected entry edge (active trip → /trips/[slug]/now) doesn't exist; the only inbound edges to Now are the bottom-nav tab and the pill toggle. Also fails the novice quality bar: the lit Now tab asserts she's somewhere she isn't, on the first screen of every trip day.
new_findings.fix: Redirect /trips/[slug] to /trips/[slug]/now when the trip is active and no planning override is in effect (or derive mode from URL so planning URLs always show planning chrome). Make getActiveTab return no active tab instead of 'now' for non-tab paths, and unify the trip-mode home (/now) between the overview button and the pill.

new_findings.title: Every visible back affordance in Trip Mode exits to a planning surface (or the trips list)
new_findings.note: Unverified (2 finders) but consistent with the verified WP-A-003 amendment: back-chevron policy, not a one-file bug.
new_findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/[itemId]/+page.svelte:37-41,54 (item-detail back → /days/[dayId] planning day view); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/today/+page.svelte:39-45 (Today back → /trips/[slug] planning home); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/documents/+page.svelte:65 (Documents back → /trips, leaves the trip entirely); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/new/+page.svelte:57 (new-item back → planning home)
new_findings.what: Charter scenarios 18/23/27: the core Trip-Mode loop is glance → tap card → item detail (address, confirmation code) → go back. The detail page's only visible back affordance is a hardcoded chevron to the planning day view; from Today the chevron goes to the planning overview; from the Documents tab it goes to the trips list, abandoning the trip. In standalone PWA display (manifest.webmanifest: display 'standalone') there is no browser back button, and the iOS edge-swipe is a hidden gesture — per the novice lens it doesn't exist. So every drill-down round-trip strands her on a planning surface under trip chrome (with 'Now' falsely lit per nav-tabs.ts:48), on the highest-frequency action of the whole trip. Recovery is re-tapping Today/Now each time.
new_findings.why: M2 — one-way streets out of Trip Mode on the primary read path; V3 flavor: the planning↔trip-day seam leaks on every item view. Would she give up? After the third teleport to a drag-handle day view she stops tapping cards and texts the group for the address.
new_findings.fix: Make backHref mode-aware: pages shared between modes (item detail, items/new) should return to the trip-mode referrer (/now or /today) when the trip is active and the visit originated there — e.g. honor a ?from=trip param or derive from AppShell mode. Documents' back should go to the trip, not /trips; Today, as a root tab, arguably needs no back chevron at all.

new_findings.title: Done-counters everywhere in Trip Mode, but nothing in Trip Mode can mark an item done
new_findings.where: src/lib/trip-mode/components/TodayTimeline.svelte:20-21,56-60 ('N of M done' pill); src/lib/trip-mode/now-state.ts:81-87 (wrapped-summary counts status==='done'); src/lib/trip-mode/components/NowDayWrapped.svelte:17-23 ('X of Y items completed'); only writer of status mid-trip is the full edit form's status field (items/[itemId]/edit)
new_findings.what: Today's header pill and the 8pm 'Day wrapped' Focus both report done-counts, but `status: 'done'` is assigned at Closeout by design (CONTEXT.md Item Status / Closeout) — no card, detail page, or sheet in Trip Mode offers a done/check-off action (TodayItemCard has only an edit pencil; the approved TRIP_REPLANNING_PRD adds skip, not done). So for a normal group the counters read '0 of 6 done' all day and the day closes with 'Day wrapped — 0 of 6 items completed', a small demoralizing lie at the exact moment the day should feel complete. A novice reads it as the app failing to notice the day happened. The only way to move the number is opening each item's full planning edit form and changing a status dropdown — not a real affordance.
new_findings.why: V3 — the trip-day→record seam: the UI promises an in-the-moment record of what got done while the model defers that record to closeout; surfacing the projection without any writer drops the data at the seam. (Not covered by any PRD: TRIP_REPLANNING_PRD's wrapped state keeps the count, adds no done-marking.)
new_findings.fix: Pick a side: either add a lightweight 'done' affordance in Trip Mode (one-tap check on TodayItemCard/item detail writing status='done' — closeout still owns considered/skip verdicts), or stop counting status mid-trip and rephrase the wrapped summary around elapsed items ('6 things on today's plan — see Today').

new_findings.title: Trip Mode activation ignores trip timezone — flips to Planning mid-evening on the trip's last day

verified.summary: Contradiction is real and exactly as described: activation.ts derives 'today' from now.toISOString() (UTC) and TripDateInfo carries no timezone, while trips/+page.server.ts groups On-trip/Past using the tz-aware tripToday(tripTz(trip)). For a negative-UTC-offset trip the final local evening flips isTripActive false (~6-8pm), AppShell hard-forces mode to 'planning' (mode = active ? … : 'planning'), wipes userOverride, and unmounts the ModePill ({#if active}), so trip chrome (Now/Today/Add) is gone while /trips simultaneously says 'On trip'; documents' on-trip gating uses the same broken predicate. Mirror early-activation the evening before day one also follows. One precision fix: now/ DOES have a +page.server.ts — it merely contains no active-gate, so the substance (/now stays URL-only-reachable, no chrome edge) is correct. P1 per charter: a mid-trip (final-evening) dead end where D3's today-scoped tasks lose their Trip-Mode home; activation.test.ts has no timezone case.
verified.evidence: src/lib/trip-mode/activation.ts:3-15 (UTC date, no tz field); src/lib/shell/trip-time.ts:44-47 tripToday(tz); src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.server.ts:30-48 tz-aware grouping; src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte:35-43 (mode forced, override wiped), :76-78 ({#if active} ModePill); documents/+page.svelte:20 onTrip = isTripActive(data.trip); grep isTripActive|redirect|active in now/+page.server.ts → no guard; grep tz in activation.test.ts → none.
verified.amendment: Minor wording only: 'now has no +page.server.ts guard' should read 'now/+page.server.ts exists but contains no active-check' — the file exists, the guard doesn't. Claim substance and severity unchanged.

new_findings.where: all /trips/[slug]/* chrome — src/lib/trip-mode/activation.ts:11 (`now.toISOString()`), src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte:35-44 vs :60, src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.server.ts:30-47, src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/documents/+page.svelte:20
new_findings.what: isTripActive() compares trip dates against the UTC calendar date (`now.toISOString().split('T')[0]`), while every other 'today' computation is trip-timezone-aware via tripToday(tripTz(trip)) (trip-time.ts). AppShell mixes both in one component: line 35 derives mode from UTC, line 60 derives todayDayId from trip tz. For any negative-UTC trip (all of the Americas), the UTC date rolls over mid-evening local: a Denver trip (UTC-6) loses Trip Mode at 18:00 on its final day — ModePill disappears (AppShell:76-80), userOverride is wiped (AppShell:42-44), nav reverts to planning tabs — while dinner and the evening plan are still ahead. Symmetrically, Trip Mode activates at ~18:00 the evening BEFORE day 1. Meanwhile the trips list (tz-aware, +page.server.ts:30-35) still shows 'On trip · Now', directly contradicting the in-trip chrome. Documents' offline `onTrip` gate (documents/+page.svelte:20) cuts out early the same way.
new_findings.why: V3 seam-drop: the trip-day→record seam fires hours early, dropping the live execution surface during the trip — a mid-trip dead end on the final evening (charter P1 wording). Also mechanical: two contradictory 'today' predicates in the same shell.
new_findings.fix: Make isTripActive take the trip's timezone: replace `now.toISOString().split('T')[0]` with `tripToday(tripTz(trip), now)` from src/lib/shell/trip-time.ts (activation.test.ts gains tz fixture cases). One-line predicate change unifies AppShell, documents gate, and trips-list bucketing.

new_findings.title: Clone is unusable: the default path 500s on duplicate days; the phases-off path creates invisible ghost items

verified.summary: Every mechanism reproduced in code. The trips after-create hook generates a day record for EVERY date in range unconditionally (day generation is not gated on created_by), so the clone action's manual day creates — whose first shifted date is exactly targetStart by construction (offset = targetStart − sourceStart) — always violate the unique (trip,date) index; the catch has no cleanup, leaving trip + owner membership + phases as retry-accumulating debris (unique slug suffix means no collision blocking retries). include_phases is checked by default, so the default submit always fails for any real trip (the hook guarantees every trip has day rows). Phases-off path verified too: items forced status:'planned' with day:''/phase:'' are invisible on every surface (day-filtered views, phase-filtered parking lot — the trip-wide parking lot is a 303 redirect to /phases — booking projection needs requires_booking which isn't copied, and items/ has no index +page). 'flight' is a real ItemType missing from the clone checklist. P2 right per bar: scenario 36 shipped-but-unusable, workaround is rebuilding by hand. Nit: it's a fail(500) form error surfacing the PB message, not a literal 500 page.
verified.evidence: clone/+page.server.ts:69-81 (create without created_by), :83-88 (manual membership — no membership collision), :110-125 (manual day creates via shiftDate → 'YYYY-MM-DD 00:00:00.000Z'), :136-165 (status:'planned', day/phase fallback '', no requires_booking), :191-195 (catch → fail, no cleanup). backend/pb_hooks/trips.pb.js:22-72 (day generation ungated). backend/pb_migrations/0005_days.js:17 unique (trip,date). clone/+page.svelte:14 (itemTypes omits 'flight'; src/lib/itinerary/types.ts:38 includes it), :99 (checked). parking-lot/+page.server.ts → redirect 303 /phases; phases/[phaseId]/+page.server.ts:32 phase-filtered; ls items/ → only [itemId], new. tests/e2e/m6-polish.spec.ts:20-31 render-only.

new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/clone — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/clone/+page.server.ts:69-81 (trip create), :110-125 (manual day creates), :136-165 (items forced to status 'planned' with day/phase possibly ''); backend/pb_hooks/trips.pb.js:22-72 (after-create hook auto-generates a day for EVERY date in range); backend/pb_migrations/0005_days.js:17 (`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_days_trip_date ON days (trip, date)`); clone/+page.svelte:99 (include_phases checked by default)
new_findings.what: When clone creates the new trip, the trips after-create hook immediately generates day records for the entire target date range. The action then manually creates shifted copies of the source days — the first one (sourceStart→targetStart) always lands on a hook-created date, violating the unique (trip,date) index → the whole action throws → 'Failed to clone trip.' Since include_phases is checked by default, the DEFAULT submit always fails, and the half-created trip (trip + owner membership + phases) is left behind in the trips list as debris; each retry adds another. Unchecking phases 'succeeds' but every cloned item is written status='planned' with day='' and phase='' — invisible in day views (day filter), parking lot (status=unplanned filter), and the booking list (requires_booking not copied, defaults false; no all-items route exists: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items has no +page). Also the type checklist omits 'flight' (clone/+page.svelte:14), so flight items can never be cloned. E2E only asserts the page renders (tests/e2e/m6-polish.spec.ts:21-31) — never submits.
new_findings.why: M3: feature shipped but its outcome is unreachable — scenario 36 ('start next year's trip from this one') cannot complete in any configuration. Workaround is rebuilding by hand / the old Doc (P2 bar). Error path is also M2: failed clone strands a phantom trip with no cleanup.
new_findings.fix: Stop manually creating days: let the hook generate them, then re-link (the phases.pb.js create hook already re-buckets hook days into the new phases). Map source items to the hook-created day with the matching shifted date. Preserve source status (unplanned stays unplanned so the parking lot carries over) or clamp planned-without-day to unplanned; copy requires_booking; add 'flight' to the type list. Wrap the whole clone in cleanup-on-failure (delete the new trip in catch). Add one submitting E2E.

new_findings.title: Export→Import round-trip is broken: real export files fail on malformed dates, and the after-create hook collides with import's explicit membership/day creates

verified.summary: Both breaks verified independently. (1) buildTripExport copies trip.start_date/end_date and d.date raw — PB returns 'YYYY-MM-DD 00:00:00.000Z' (the codebase's own split(/[T ]/) calls evidence the stored format) — and validateTripImport checks only typeof string with no normalization, so import appends another ' 00:00:00.000Z' producing an unparseable date and the trip create throws: a genuine Waypoint export can never import. (2) With hand-normalized dates, import passes created_by so the trips hook creates the owner membership and all day rows; import's explicit trip_members create then violates unique (trip,user) (and its day creates would violate unique (trip,date)); the catch does no cleanup, so trip + hook membership + hook days persist as an owned phantom trip, repeated per retry. The path is advertised on the trips list in both empty and non-empty states, and E2E only asserts the file input renders. P2 fits: the 'JSON backup' cannot be restored, scenario 37's value void, workaround is the old stack.
verified.evidence: src/lib/portability/export.ts:21-22,43 raw dates; src/lib/portability/import.ts:27-42 (validateTripImport, no date handling); trips/import/+page.server.ts:42-43,85 (' 00:00:00.000Z' appended), :51 (created_by), :55-61 (explicit membership), :146-150 (catch, no cleanup). backend/pb_hooks/trips.pb.js:7-72; backend/pb_migrations/0003_trip_members.js:21 unique (trip,user) WHERE user != ''; 0005_days.js:17. trips/+page.svelte:56-58,141-145 'import from JSON'. tests/e2e/m5-closure.spec.ts:52-57 render-only; no submitting import/clone E2E exists.

new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/export + /trips/import — src/lib/portability/export.ts (raw `trip.start_date`/`d.date` like '2026-06-12 00:00:00.000Z'), src/routes/(app)/trips/import/+page.server.ts:42-43 & 85 (appends ' 00:00:00.000Z' again → '…00:00:00.000Z 00:00:00.000Z'), :51-61 (created_by set → hook creates owner membership, then explicit trip_members create), backend/pb_hooks/trips.pb.js:7-20 & 22-72, backend/pb_migrations/0003_trip_members.js:21 (unique (trip,user)), src/lib/portability/import.ts:29-32 (no date normalization)
new_findings.what: Two independent breaks. (1) Export emits PB's stored datetime strings; import concatenates another ' 00:00:00.000Z' onto them, producing an invalid date — so importing a genuine Waypoint export fails at the trip create. (2) Even with hand-normalized YYYY-MM-DD dates, the trips after-create hook fires (import passes created_by) and creates the owner membership AND all day records; import then explicitly creates the same membership (unique idx violation) and the same days (unique (trip,date) violation). The catch returns 'Failed to import trip.' but the trip + hook membership + hook days already exist — the user now owns a phantom half-trip, and every retry mints another. This is the advertised first-run path ('or import from JSON', src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.svelte:56-58 and :141-145). E2E only checks the file input renders (tests/e2e/m5-closure.spec.ts:52-56). Net: the More page's 'Download trip as JSON backup' is a backup that cannot be restored — scenario 37's value is void.
new_findings.why: M3 shipped-but-unreachable outcome + M2 one-way error street (failed import leaves un-cleanupable debris in the trips list). Fails V1 in effect: the safety-net for trip data is the old stack again.
new_findings.fix: Normalize dates on import (substring(0,10)) or emit date-only in export (bump _waypoint_version); drop import's explicit trip_members create (the hook owns it); reuse hook-generated days by date instead of creating new ones; delete the trip in the catch block. Add a round-trip E2E: export a seeded trip, import the file, assert day/item counts.

new_findings.title: Share-with-grandma dead-ends: archive 'link' is an untappable relative path with no copy button, and the publish rules shown to the owner are wrong
new_findings.note: Verified via WP-A-019 (confirmed P2); adds the finishCloseout-mints-token-silently half.
new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/settings + /archive/[token] — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.svelte:167-174 (`/archive/{token}`, no anchor/copy/origin), :125-127 ('link is generated after the trip is archived' — actually generated on enable, +page.server.ts:106-109), :153-154 ('Publish delay (days after archiving)'), src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:28-35 (delay computed from END DATE; archived=true bypasses delay entirely), src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/closeout/+page.server.ts:118 (finish redirects to unchanged trip home)
new_findings.what: Scenario 35 walked as the novice owner: More→Settings→'Enable public archive'→Save. The 'Share URL' appears as a code-styled relative path — not a link, not tappable, no copy button, no domain. She cannot produce a working URL without mentally prepending the origin and hand-copying from a code block. Every label around it is also false: copy says the link is generated 'after the trip is archived' (it's minted the moment she enables the toggle); the delay field says 'days after archiving' but the route publishes at end_date+N regardless of archiving, and archiving publishes IMMEDIATELY, skipping the delay. So if she enables mid-planning and texts the path, grandma gets a bare 404 'Archive not yet published' with no explanation on either side of when it will work. And after the one moment the link is certainly live — tapping 'Finish & Archive Trip' — she is redirected to the unchanged trip home with no confirmation and no share surface; the link stays buried under Settings.
new_findings.why: V1: at the exact share moment, the only workable move is the old stack (text photos / screenshots). Plus M4: expected share affordance (copyable absolute URL + post-archive 'share it' moment) missing.
new_findings.fix: Render the full absolute URL as an anchor with a Copy button (navigator.clipboard + toast, matching join-link UI on Members). Fix the copy: 'link goes live when you archive, or N days after the trip ends.' After finishCloseout, land on (or banner into) a 'Trip archived — share the record' state exposing the same copy control.

new_findings.title: Archiving is an unguarded one-way door: completable from day 0 in three taps, irreversible — no unarchive exists anywhere

verified.summary: Every cited mechanism verified by content. Closeout card on More is gated only by isOwnerOrCoOwner (more/+page.svelte ~54-70) — no date/status condition; finishCloseout (closeout/+page.server.ts:98-123) sets archived:true with no end-date/status guard and the 'Finish & Archive Trip' button (closeout/+page.svelte ~146-153) has no confirm dialog and no consequence copy; archived hard-disables Trip Mode (activation.ts:10); once archived the public gate is bypassed (archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:33), so archiving can instantly publish; the trips-list active bucket filters purely by dates, never consulting archived (trips/+page.server.ts:30-44). Repo-wide, archived:false appears only at new:62/import:52/clone:80, no unarchive action or route exists, and no Svelte UI renders an archived indicator (only settings prose at :126). Bonus evidence the verifier found: settings/+page.server.ts:124-150 contains a dead archiveTrip action no form invokes — a third unguarded archive write with zero UI. Not deferred: D5 documents the missing wrap-up state, not the absence of guard/unarchive/badge. P2 (M2 one-way street) is right — P1's 'archive front-door failure' refers to the public surface, and the action requires deliberate owner taps.
verified.evidence: more/+page.svelte:54-70 (isOwnerOrCoOwner-only Card); closeout/+page.server.ts:98-123; closeout/+page.svelte:146-153; activation.ts:10; archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:33; trips/+page.server.ts:30-44 (date-only buckets); grep archived: → false only new:62/import:52/clone:80, true in closeout:111 + settings:137 (dead action, no form at settings page actions ?/update ?/toggleArchive ?/delete); grep -i unarchive → zero hits; grep archived in *.svelte → only settings copy line 126.

new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/closeout + /trips/[slug]/settings — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte:54-70 (closeout card always visible to owners, any trip status/date), closeout/+page.server.ts:98-123 (finishCloseout sets archived=true, no date/status guard), closeout/+page.svelte:147-154 ('Finish & Archive Trip', no consequence copy, no confirm), src/lib/trip-mode/activation.ts:10 (archived hard-disables Trip Mode), src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:33 (archived publishes the public link immediately), src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.server.ts:30-35 (active bucket ignores archived)
new_findings.what: The Closeout card sits on More from the moment a trip is created. An owner can walk the wizard and tap 'Finish & Archive Trip' before the trip even starts; nothing explains what archiving does (kills Trip Mode forever, may instantly publish the public link). Once archived=true, NO route, action, or setting can flip it back — grep shows `archived: false` only at create time (trips/new:62, clone:80, import:52); Settings has no unarchive control. An accidental archive permanently demotes a live trip: AppShell will never enter Trip Mode (activation.ts:10) while the trips list still files it under 'On trip · Now' (its bucketing ignores archived), and the only 'recovery' is the broken export→import path. Also nothing visible marks the trip as archived afterward — Settings, trip home, and the Past card all render identically.
new_findings.why: M2 one-way street: a state-changing action with no way back and no error-path escape; for the novice persona an exploratory tap is unrecoverable. Compounded by M4 (no archived indicator anywhere in members' UI).
new_findings.fix: Guard the door: confirm dialog on Finish that states consequences; hide or soft-warn on the closeout card until end_date is near (ties into the D5 wrap-up PRD). Add an 'Unarchive trip' action in Settings (owner-gated, clears archived). Show an 'Archived' badge on trip home, Settings, and the Past card; make the trips-list active bucket exclude archived.

new_findings.title: Archive publish gate contradicts settings copy: delay measured from end_date not archiving, bypassed entirely once archived, and trips auto-publish without closeout

verified.summary: Gate logic matches the claim exactly: archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:28-34 computes publishDate = end_date + (archive_publish_after_days || 7) and gates with `if (!trip.archived && now < publishDate)` — so (a) the delay is measured from end_date, not archiving (no archived_at exists anywhere), (b) once archived the delay is skipped entirely, making the 'Publish delay (days after archiving)' knob (settings/+page.svelte:153-155) a no-op on the normal closeout flow, and (c) an enabled-but-never-archived trip auto-publishes at end_date+delay, contradicting the copy 'link is generated after the trip is archived' (settings/+page.svelte:126). Token is minted at enable-time in toggleArchive (settings/+page.server.ts:106-109), and buildArchiveView maps status='planned' into consideredItems (archive-view.ts:36-38) while sanitization retains title/location_name/location_address — so the un-closed-out planned itinerary does render publicly under 'What we considered'. P2 right: code-verified guard-vs-copy breach, not a critical-path dead end.
verified.evidence: archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:24-35 (enable check + gate); settings/+page.svelte:126,153-155 (copy); settings/+page.server.ts:106-109 (mint at enable); closeout/+page.server.ts:111-115 (mint at archive); archive-view.ts:8-21 (sanitizer keeps title/locations) + 36-38 (consideredItems includes 'planned'); no archived_at field anywhere.

new_findings.where: src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:28-34 (publishDate = end_date + archive_publish_after_days; gate is `if (!trip.archived && now < publishDate)`) vs src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.svelte:126 ('a public read-only link is generated after the trip is archived') and :153-155 ('Publish delay (days after archiving)'); token actually minted at enable-time in settings/+page.server.ts:106-109; src/lib/portability/archive-view.ts:36-38 (consideredItems includes status='planned')
new_findings.what: The label promises two things the code does not do. (1) 'Days after archiving' is computed from end_date — and if the trip IS archived, the delay is skipped completely, so in the normal closeout flow the link is live the second the owner taps 'Finish & Archive'; the delay knob has no effect on the path it claims to govern. (2) 'Generated after the trip is archived' — the token is generated when the checkbox is enabled, and the page goes public at end_date+delay even if closeout/archiving never happens; an owner who enabled the toggle pre-trip and never closed out has their full planned itinerary (titles, locations, addresses) publicly rendered under 'What we considered' (archive-view.ts maps leftover 'planned' into consideredItems) 7 days after the trip ends, without ever consenting to publish.
new_findings.why: M3/guard-vs-chrome — the shipped publish-delay control is unreachable-in-effect on the normal flow, and the documented publish precondition ('after archived') is not enforced by the gate. Owner-expectation breach is code-verified, not vibes.
new_findings.fix: Pick one model and make copy match code: gate on `trip.archived` as the hard precondition (never public before closeout), and compute publishDate from the archive timestamp (store archived_at) so the delay does what its label says. Update settings copy accordingly.

new_findings.title: Closeout never reviews parking-lot ideas — unplanned items silently vanish from the archive record

verified.summary: Both variants of the claim verify. The wizard is day-cards-then-summary only: itemsForDay filters i.day === day.id, so unplanned items (day='') never enter any step, and no phase-end review exists anywhere in the component. The loader fetches ALL trip items, so unplanned rows silently inflate the summary's else-bucket labeled 'Skipped (stayed planned)'. Downstream, buildArchiveView puts status planned|considered into consideredItems and excludes unplanned entirely — so the public 'what we considered' both drops every parking-lot idea and relabels owner-skipped planned items as considered. The documented contract says otherwise in two live docs (SPEC.md:523 + :585, CONTEXT.md:47 verbatim 'At the end of each phase, reviews unplanned items…bulk auto-consider'), and V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD:129/:246 sequence the deferred goals step 'after the per-phase unplanned review' — assuming it exists. SPEC_BACKLOG's Archive & Portability backlog contains only the memories-stay-private resolution; nothing defers this step. The claim's own deferral hygiene is correct (goals review and memory review properly cited as deferred, not flagged). V3 seam-drop + live-doc lie; P2 is right — the wizard runs and the archive publishes, data stays in the DB, so it under-reports the record rather than breaking a critical path (P1's bar).
verified.evidence: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/closeout/+page.svelte:17-28 (sortedDays + itemsForDay day-filter), :30-40 (summary: else planned++ catches unplanned), :74-111 (day cards → summary, no phase step), :123 ('Skipped (stayed planned)' label); closeout/+page.server.ts:12-15 (items filter trip-wide, includes unplanned); src/lib/portability/archive-view.ts:35-38 (doneItems=done; consideredItems=planned||considered; unplanned excluded); docs/SPEC.md:523,585; CONTEXT.md:47; docs/V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD.md:129,246; docs/SPEC_BACKLOG.md:133-137 (no deferral).

new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/closeout + /archive/[token] — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/closeout/+page.svelte:17-28 (wizard iterates days only; itemsForDay matches item.day) and :30-40 (summary buckets done/planned/considered — unplanned lumped invisibly into 'planned'), src/lib/itinerary/components/CloseoutDayCard.svelte:35-37, src/lib/portability/archive-view.ts:34-38 (doneItems=done; consideredItems=planned|considered; unplanned excluded)
new_findings.what: Unplanned items have no day, so the day-by-day wizard never surfaces them: there is no end-of-phase 'keep for archive or remove' step and no bulk auto-consider. SPEC.md:523 and :585 spec exactly that step ('At end of each phase, reviews unplanned items: keep for archive (mark considered) or remove, with bulk auto-consider'), CONTEXT.md's Closeout definition still claims it, and V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD.md US13 assumes it exists ('a goal-review step after the per-phase unplanned review') — but it is not in code and not listed as deferred in SPEC_BACKLOG (Archive & Portability backlog covers only the memories-stay-private resolution). Result: every idea the group collected but didn't schedule is excluded from the public archive's 'What we considered' section (archive-view filters to planned|considered), so the keepsake record under-reports what the group actually considered. Note: the MISSING GOALS review step in the same wizard is documented-deferred (V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD.md:34-35, 241-244 — 'rides with a future closeout deep-dive') and is cited here, not flagged; the memory-review step is likewise deferred (TRIP_MEMORY_PRD shelved, SPEC_BACKLOG Trip Memory: 'Shipped: Nothing').
new_findings.why: V3 seam-drop at trip-day→record: data the group created (ideas) is dropped crossing into the record. Also M4 vs SPEC.md/CONTEXT.md which both claim the review exists (live-doc lie → D6 hygiene note).
new_findings.fix: Add the spec'd phase-end review screen to the wizard: list that phase's unplanned items with keep-as-considered / remove / 'consider all' bulk action (the deferred goals step can slot after it later, per the PRD's own ordering). Until built, correct CONTEXT.md's Closeout entry to match code.

new_findings.title: Budget page never compares the plan's cost estimates to the budget

verified.summary: Code-true: the budget loader fetches trip_budgets and expenses only — never items — and grandTotal is the sum of budget category envelopes yet is labeled 'Estimated Total'. The plan's cost_estimate_usd aggregates nowhere above a single day: the only Σ in the repo is day-card.ts's per-day budgetTotal (shown via DayMetricToggle chips), confirmed by repo-wide grep of cost_estimate_usd usage. Not deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG Money backlog lists only multi-currency (off-table) and item↔expense nav; no PRD defers a plan-vs-budget rollup. This is charter scenario 8 verbatim ('Can we afford this? — budget status vs plan') failing V1 — the spreadsheet survives — so P2 fits exactly. One citation nuance: CARD_CONTENT_SPEC's 'never aggregated anywhere' row refers to cost_actual_usd (the estimate 'circulates' in day chips), but the substantive claim stands on code alone.

new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/budget — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/budget/+page.server.ts:13-56 (loads trip_budgets + expenses only, never items/cost_estimate_usd), budget/+page.svelte:102-103 (the budget envelope itself is labeled 'Estimated Total'), src/lib/itinerary/day-card.ts:40 (Σ cost_estimate_usd exists only as per-day chips behind the DayMetricToggle on Overview/Phase)
new_findings.what: During planning the Money tab can't answer 'can we afford this plan?': Budget shows category limits vs money SPENT (expenses), which is ~$0 before the trip, while the plan's Item Cost estimates — CONTEXT.md's forward-looking 'what we expect to pay' — aggregate nowhere above a single day. To compare plan to budget a member must open every day, toggle the metric, and sum chips by hand. The 'Estimated Total' heading actually showing the budget envelope compounds the confusion.
new_findings.why: V1 (forces the old stack): the cost-tally spreadsheet stays alive for exactly the question the Budget page exists for. Not deferred: SPEC_BACKLOG Money lists only multi-currency (off-table) and item↔expense navigation; CARD_CONTENT_SPEC.md:32 itself flags that cost is 'never aggregated anywhere' as a defect rationale, and its deferred list covers expense-nav, not a plan-vs-budget rollup.
new_findings.fix: One items fetch in the budget loader (same field-limited query the Overview uses) to sum cost_estimate_usd per expense category; render a second bar or number per category — 'planned $X / budget $Y / spent $Z' — and rename the header to distinguish budget from plan estimate.

new_findings.title: No trip-wide way to find a specific item ('when is our flight?')
new_findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/ contains only new/ and [itemId]/ (no index), trips/[slug]/+page.svelte:151-158 + src/lib/itinerary/day-card.ts (Overview day cards expose counts, booked/budget chips and a lodging stay chip — never item titles/types), lists/booking (+page.svelte:59-78) surfaces only planned-but-unbooked items
new_findings.what: Charter scenario 1, the first novice task: from trip home there is no surface listing items by type or title. Days are the only index; finding the flight means remembering its date and tapping that day. The booking Smart List drops the flight once it's booked, Documents helps only if a file was attached. On a 14-day trip the worst case is opening days one by one.

Kept P3 because workable in-app paths exist (flights are usually day 1/last, Documents, booking list) and CARD_CONTENT_SPEC documents the day-card minimalism as a decision — the gap is the missing trip-level lens, not the cards.
new_findings.fix: A small 'Key details' or type-filtered item lens — e.g. flights/lodging rows on the trip Overview stats card, or a lightweight items index reachable from the Itinerary sub-tabs — rather than full search.

new_findings.title: Item detail back-link and delete redirect eject day-less (parking) items to trip Overview, not their phase
new_findings.where: /trips/[slug]/items/[itemId] — src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/items/[itemId]/+page.svelte:37-41 (backHref: day ? day page : trip Overview — itemPhase ignored); items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts:86-87 (itemPhase is loaded and returned), :128-131 (delete: day ? day page : Overview); same pattern in items/[itemId]/edit/+page.server.ts:192-195
